mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crossing above 4.5% is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) are elevated simultaneously, the monetary stance is highly restrictive, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reducing forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If periods with CPIAUCSL > 310 and FEDFUNDS > 5% produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a mature expansion characterized by high retail sales (RSAFS), a sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator of economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST has fallen for two consecutive months
regime_hint: mature expansion with high retail sales
failure_condition: If during periods of RSAFS above its trailing 12-month median, a two-month drop in HOUST does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns with a win rate above 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and rising incomes, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are associated with negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the year-over-year change in CPI, real interest rates turn positive and rising. This tight financial condition increases the equity risk premium and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If periods with positive real rates show average 60-day SPX returns higher than periods with negative real rates, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) — more than 1% month-over-month — signals imminent consumer spending weakness and a possible demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: If months with RSAFS decline > 1% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with falling payrolls (PAYEMS) signals productivity gains that reduce unit labor costs and ease inflation pressure, boosting equity valuations over the short term.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month while PAYEMS decreases
failure_condition: If during CPIAUCSL > 310, the divergence condition is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.
